title: CMS Posts Preliminary Payment Determination for New Category I CPT Code Covering Use of OGM for Constitutional Genetic Disorders
text: Bionano Laboratories announced that the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services (CMS) posted a preliminary payment determination for CPT code 81354, which covers optical genome mapping (OGM) for detecting structural and copy number variations related to constitutional genetic disorders. The CMS crosswalked the new code to an existing proprietary laboratory analyte code priced at $1,263.53—substantially higher than comparable microarray codes 81228 ($900) and 81229 ($1,160). This pricing structure takes effect January 1, 2026. According to Alka Chaubey, chief medical officer of Bionano, Pricing at this level is consistent with the needs of laboratories today seeking to move forward from legacy methods. The code enables reimbursement for tests like OGM-Dx Postnatal Whole Genome SV and OGM-Dx Prenatal Whole Genome SV, which address developmental delay, intellectual disabilities, and autism spectrum disorder. This represents the second Category I CPT code for OGM technology, following code 81195 for hematologic malignancy analysis, positioning optical genome mapping as a viable alternative to traditional cytogenetics methods.
